sections.heading: 1. Preperation
sections.body:
- Boil water in a pot - as much as you want to drink or save :p
* Before you boil the water, put the Dashi packet into the water for good flavor!

- Cut vegetables into bite sizes while the water boils

sections.heading: 2. Throw 'em in
sections.body:
- Toss everything into the pot, let it boil for 5+ minutes; if you want a more fresher feel to the vegetables you can boil them for shorter time!

- When the water looks golden, you can remove the Dashi packet out of the soup

- Cut Tofu

sections.heading: 3. Miso & Finish
sections.body:
- Once the vegetables seem cooked enough, put Tofu in and stop the fire

- Scoop up some Miso with ladle, dip it into the pot, and gently let the Miso dissolve slowly using chopsticks or other utensils.

* A good guideline is to look at the soup's color - when it stops looking watery, the soup is about right! Always sip while adding more to get to the right balance for you :)

ingredients.amount: I recommend bok choy or napa cabbage!
A dash (Don't do too much like I would :[ )

sections.heading: 1. Preparation
sections.body:
- Boil water, and while the water heats up put 1 Dashi packet into the pot for base flavor :)

- If pairing with noodles that take a long time to cook, boil water in a separate pot too!

- Cut veggies

sections.heading: 2. Cook & Soup Base
sections.body:
- Put the veggies into the pot as the water is boiling, harder items first :)

- Add the flavors: for the big pot with ~2L water, I would start with 3 tbsp Shoyu, 2 tbsp Mirin, 1 tbsp Sake, and 2 tbsp Tsuyu! Optionally you can add spicy flavors, such as Shichimi, Gochujang, etc.!

- Add tofu shortly before the veggies are cooked and you are stopping the fire

sections.heading: Enjoy!
sections.body:
- Stop the fire whenever the veggies are cooked to your liking!

- Spread a dash of Sesame oil, and enjoy :)

title: たまごスープ / Egg Tomato Soup

sections.heading: 1. Preparation
sections.body:
- Boil water

- Cut tomatoes into bitable sizes

- Break & mix up the eggs (don't whisk too much :) )

- Cut tofu into small bites

- Semi-mince green onions (do not have to be too small - up to you!)

sections.heading: 2. Tomato & Soup base
sections.body:
- Put tomato in as the water is boiling, cook for ~5 minutes

- Add 3 tbsp Shoyu, 3 tbsp Sake, ginger, and 2 tbsp Tsuyu (this one is the heaviest flavor, so add little by little to adjust!)

sections.heading: 3. Wrap up and enjoy!
sections.body:
- Put green onions in shortly before stopping the fire

- After the fire stops and the soup stops bubbling, add the whisked egg into the soup and let it be cooked

- Add a dash of sesame oil, briefly mix the soup around, and done!

title: タンタンメン / Dandan noodle (for 2 big servings!)

sections.heading: 1. Cook the ingredients (if you want the lazy way, just do the pan part in the same soup pot :] )
sections.body:
- Boil 1.5L water in kettle

- Boil plenty water in a separate pot for noodles

- Cut the vegetables into preferred sizes

- Put pan on medium heat (if going the lazy way, use the soup pot from here on)

- Put in:
    - a dash of sesame oil, 
    - 1 tbsp Shoyu,
    - 1 tbsp Toubanjan, 
    - 1 tbsp garlic, 
    - 2 ginger cubes, 
    - dash of honey

- put onion, then veggies, then tofu!

- Once cooked enough, heat off and let it sit a while

sections.heading: 2. Noodle Prep
sections.body:
- Put the amount of noodle you want Into the noodle pot

- keep a timer :)

- whenever it's done, drain the water, and wash the noodle to get rid of the slimey texture on the cooked noodle!

sections.heading: 3. Soup Prep
sections.body:
- (If we are doing the lazy way, the ingredients should already be sitting and cooked in this pot!)

- Put 1.5L kettle-boiled water into a big pot (should not be actively boiling :] )

- Add:
    - 8 tbsp miso, 
    - 1 -2 Dashi packet,
    - 1 tbsp Tsuyu
    - 900 mL / 4 cups of oat milk
 and heat until briefly boiled

- Then stop the fire, sprinkle the ground sesame seeds, and let it rest!

sections.heading: Enjoy!
sections.body: Serve the amount you like, and enjoy!

sections.heading: 1. Preparation
sections.body:
- Wash and cut up ingredients

- Whisk eggs

sections.heading: 2. Cook the egg
sections.body:
- Pan in medium heat; put a generous amount of olive oil (in general, the more oil the fluffier the eggs become :) )

- Grab a bowl or container for egg's escape

- put in the eggs; watch as it hardens and fluffs up; gently break apart the egg into smaller pieces, and before it completely hardens  (a bit runny here and there is good), put it away into a bowl

sections.heading: 3. Tomato time
sections.body:
- Put the cut up tomatoes into the same pan

- Add a lil bit of water (the more water you add, the soupier and also milder taste)

- Add 3 tbsp Shoyu, 2 tbsp Mirin, dash of honey

- let it cook, occasionally stir

sections.heading: Combine & enjoy
sections.body:
- Put the eggs back into the pan with tomatoes in it

- Mix up well, then put green onions if you have them :)

Enjoy!

title: ヴィーガンじゃが / Vegan Japanese Potato Stew

sections.heading: 1. Preparation
sections.body: - Cut the potatoes and carrots into small blocks; onions into small long pieces

sections.heading: 2. Fry 'em in a pot
sections.body:
- Cook onions in a pot till it's golden. Use half a cup of water instead of oil for more hardcore vegan like your mom :p

- Put the cut up potatoes and carrots into the pot

sections.heading: 3. Stewwww
sections.body:
- Put water into the pot, so that the ingredients are mostly simmering in the water (few corners and bits poking up is good)

- Add: 
    - 4 tbsp Mirin
    - 3 tbsp Shoyu
    - 3 tbsp Sake
    - 1 tbsp honey

- Let it cook for 20-30 minutes!

sections.heading: Enjoy!
sections.body: When the ingredients are super soft and chopsticks can easily puncture them, it's good to go!

sections.heading: 1. Preparation
sections.body:
- Wash and cut the veggies

- heat up the pan in medium~high heat

sections.heading: 2. Cook & Go
sections.body:
- Put a dash of olive oil into the pan

- Add 1 tbsp garlic, 2 ginger cubes (if using actual garlic, it's fun to cut them into long, thin strips!), and cook them for a little while

sections.heading: 3. Add everything
sections.body:
- Add veggies, 4 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp oyster sauce, mix and cook to your liking

Enjoy!
